body_txt: The stereotypical Near-Death Experience includes seeing a bright light, experiencing love coming from it, and sensing somehow that if you were really dead — not Only Mostly Dead — then you should go into it. Death is just the beginning, after all, and you probably had a very similar experience when you were being born. Now, a sparkly light after death is seen as the functional means by which ghosts move on, as well as the difference between Near-Death Experience and Deader than Dead . Entire plots have been built around convincing ghosts to Go Into the Light. Psychopomp-style versions of The Grim Reaper help guide you there with varying degrees of force. Compare Disappears into Light . Often overlaps with Stairway to Heaven and Ending by Ascending .
